title: Rack power vs inlet temperature correlation
difficulty_justification: Requires retrieving two 30-day timeseries (power and temperature) and computing their correlation — cross-source fusion over a long window.
query_text: What is the statistical correlation between rack power draw and inlet temperature in row C over the last 30 days? Is there a significant relationship?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Pearson correlation between PDU power and inlet temperature for racks R-C04 and R-C05 over 30 days: r = 0.74 (p < 0.001), indicating a strong positive correlation. A 1 kW increase in rack power corresponds to approximately a 0.9°C rise in inlet temperature in this row. The relationship is stronger during peak compute hours (09:00–22:00 UTC). This suggests the cooling system in row C is thermally sensitive to load spikes and would benefit from load-balancing or pre-cooling during batch submission windows.

checkpoints.description: Agent retrieves both power and temperature timeseries.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains both PDU power and inlet temperature queries
final_answer contains a correlation value in [0.65, 0.85] range
